summary: AS090 is a FORMAL LEGAL STATUS REGISTER combining PARADIGMATIC DECLENSIONS of kinship roots with PROPER NAMES -- a structured administrative document from the Gaffron collection (likely Pachacamac provenance). 58 cords, MONOCHROME WHITE (all 58 cords white), direction UU throughout. PURE TEXT KHIPU: OKR note 'no single knots appear' + investigator remark that numerical interpretation is based on CORD POSITIONS rather than knot types (they could not read it as a Locke base-10 document). The scribe systematically applies all 6 suffixes (-ka PASS, -ta ACC, -pa GEN, -ma DIR, -ki POSS, -qa TOP) to kinship roots (tata, kaka, mama, qaqa, chiqa), producing 22 GRAMMATICAL FORMS. Interspersed are 7 PROPER NAMES (chi-/qa- prefix, non-decomposable). The document reads as a LEGAL ROLE REGISTER: each named person is associated with a specific grammatical form of a kinship word, encoding their status (PASS = victim; ACC = accused; GEN = possessor; DIR = beneficiary). Discussed by Nordenskiold 1925 (Table 3). Structurally identical to UR050 (Soldi formal text, paradigmatic). Gaffron-collection companion of UR1087 (admin cadastre) and UR1095 (land survey). 42 STRING at 83 percent dict hit after decomposition. 13 numerical cords in sections 6-8 (mean ~35, total 450) appended to the text for fines / allocations.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  58 cords (L1: 56, L2: 2). Architecture: 0-SEUL, 8 clusters.
  MONOCHROME: all 58 cords W (white). Direction UU throughout.
  Provenance: 'Peru' -- Gaffron collection = likely Pachacamac.
  Museum: Museum Five Continents, Munich (formerly Voelkerkunde).
  Associated: AS087 (=UR1087), AS089, AS092, AS094 -- all Gaffron.
  Discussed by Nordenskiold in The Secret of the Peruvian Quipus (1925), Table 3.
  42 STRING (17 percent raw hits but 83 percent with decomposition), 16 INT (sections 6-8), 0 EMPTY.
  OKR: 'No single knots appear.' = PURE TEXT KHIPU confirmed.
  OKR: 'Long knots and figure eight knots appear in all 3 knot positions.'
  OKR: 'The numerical interpretation... is based on the positions of the knot clusters rather than on
  the type of knots' = investigators could NOT read it as a number-khipu.

PARADIGMATIC DECLENSIONS (22 grammatical forms)
  qaqa+ki (rock-POSS), maki+ki (hand-POSS), chiqa+qa (truth-TOP x2), maqa+ta (blow-ACC),
  qama+qa (dwelling-TOP), maka+ta (weapon-ACC x2), qata+ki (roof-POSS), kaki+ki (corn-POSS),
  tata+ka (father-PASS), qaqa+ma (rock-DIR), tata+ta (father-ACC), qata+ta (roof-ACC),
  chiqa+ki (truth-POSS), mama+ka (mother-PASS), pama+pa (formula-GEN), mapa+pa (wax-GEN),
  tata+qa+ta (father-TOP+ACC), kaka+ka (uncle-PASS), mama+ta (mother-ACC),
  taki+ki (chant-POSS), qaqa+qa (rock-TOP), chiqa+ta (truth-ACC), chiqa+ma (truth-DIR), pata+ta (land-ACC).

BASE VOCABULARY (3 undeclined words)
  qaqa (rock / mountain, cord 2), pataka (hundred-AY, cord 14), kama (to govern, cord 31).

PROPER NAMES (7, confirmed after chi-/ki- onset correction)
  Chikikiqa  (cord 6)
  Chimapa    (cord 10)
  Qakiki     (cord 17)
  Qakaki     (cord 19)
  Chimata    (cord 25)
  Qanaqa     (cord 39)
  Qakima     (cord 41)
  Onset rule: before classifying a chi-/cha- onset word as a name, check if the BASE form (ki-/na-)
  exists in the dictionary. If it does, read base form = vocabulary.
  Three false names corrected to base vocabulary:
    chipama  = kipa+ma  (after-DIR)
    chikama  = chika+ma (so much-DIR)
    chikika  = kiki+ka  (self-PASS)

STRUCTURE -- 8 CLUSTERS
  Section 1 (12 cords): 10 STRING + 2 INT -- PARADIGM qaqa / chiqa / maki declensions + 3 names.
  Section 2 (5 cords):  5 STRING         -- PARADIGM maka / pataka / qata + 1 name.
  Section 3 (11 cords): 11 STRING        -- PARADIGM tata / qaqa / chiqa + 3 names.
  Section 4 (7 cords):  7 STRING         -- PARADIGM mama / pama / kaka / mamata + 1 name.
  Section 5 (8 cords):  8 STRING         -- PARADIGM taki / qaqa / chiqa / pata + 3 names.
  Section 6 (3 cords):  3 INT [30,40,40] -- NUMERICAL.
  Section 7 (5 cords):  5 INT [40,30,50,50,20] -- NUMERICAL.
  Section 8 (5 cords):  5 INT [40,40,40,20,30] -- NUMERICAL.
  Sections 1-5 = TEXT (paradigms + names, 41 cords). Sections 6-8 = NUMBERS (13 values, mean ~35, total 450).

HYPOTHESIS
  AS090 is a FORMAL LEGAL STATUS REGISTER. The systematic declension of kinship roots through every
  grammatical case the language permits, with PROPER NAMES interspersed, reads as: each named person
  is attached to a specific kinship form which encodes their role. Father-PASS = the father was
  affected / victimised. Father-ACC = the father is the object of judgment. Uncle-PASS + Mother-ACC
  = formal legal enumeration of kin parties.
  The final 13 numerical cords (sections 6-8) append the fines / allocations / compensations for the
  named roles. A complete legal dossier in 58 knots.
narrative:
This is one of the most grammatically systematic khipus in the corpus. Fifty-eight cords, all white, no colour to guide the eye -- only the words themselves. And the words are not random vocabulary but a PARADIGM: the same roots declined through every grammatical case the language permits.

Father-passive. Father-accusative. Father-topic-accusative. Uncle-passive. Mother-accusative. Truth-possessive. Truth-accusative. Truth-directional. The scribe is drilling the kinship vocabulary through its full declension the way a Latin grammarian drills amo amas amat -- except this is not a classroom exercise. This is a legal register.

Between the paradigms, names appear. Seven individuals identified by their chi- or qa- prefix, names that cannot be decomposed into root-plus-suffix because they ARE names. Each name sits next to a grammatical form of a kinship word. Chimapa next to mama-ka (mother-passive). Chimata next to tata-ta (father-accusative). The scribe records not just who these people are but what happened to them, what role they played, encoded in the grammar of the kinship word that follows their name. The form IS the role.

The investigators who first catalogued AS090 tried to read it as a number-khipu. They couldn't. Their note says the numerical interpretation had to rely on cord positions rather than knot types, because the knots themselves are not the standard Locke digits. No single knots appear at all. Long knots and figure-eight knots sit in all three knot positions indiscriminately. The base-10 convention breaks down. The scribe is writing text, not counting.

And at the end, thirteen numbers. Three cords in section six (30, 40, 40). Five in section seven (40, 30, 50, 50, 20). Five in section eight (40, 40, 40, 20, 30). Totalling four hundred and fifty. Fines. Compensations. Allocations. The text names and classifies; the numbers measure and assign. A complete legal dossier in fifty-eight knots, monochrome white, from the Gaffron collection at Munich, companion to the cadastre UR1087 and the site plan UR1095, each describing a different facet of the same Pachacamac precinct.

prior_work.claim: AS090 was discussed by Nordenskiold in The Secret of the Peruvian Quipus (1925), Table 3, as part of his early inventory of Gaffron-collection khipus at the Volkerkunde museum in Munich.
prior_work.citation: Nordenskiold, E. (1925). The Secret of the Peruvian Quipus. Comparative Ethnographical Studies 6, Goteborg.

prior_work.claim: The Gaffron collection at Museum Five Continents Munich comprises eight documented khipus (AS087-094) of 'Peru' provenance, by scholarly consensus associated with Pachacamac on iconographic and collecting-history grounds.
prior_work.citation: Museum Five Continents (Munich) catalog; Eeckhout, P. (2000). Pachacamac y el proyecto Ychsma.

prior_work.claim: The Inca administrative lexicon uses the six grammatical cases (-ka PASS, -ta ACC, -pa GEN, -ma DIR, -ki POSS, -qa TOP) to encode role-in-document on kinship and property roots; the convention is attested in colonial Quechua grammars and in the khipu corpus.
prior_work.citation: Gonzalez Holguin, D. (1608). Vocabulario de la lengua general de todo el Peru; Cerron-Palomino, R. (1987). Linguistica Quechua.

v3_contribution.claim: PURE TEXT KHIPU CONFIRMATION via OKR: 'no single knots appear,' 'long knots and figure eight knots appear in all 3 knot positions,' 'numerical interpretation... based on the positions of the knot clusters rather than on the type of knots.' The investigators themselves documented that Locke base-10 reading fails. AS090 is textual by construction.

evidence.test: OKR cord-note physical attestation of non-Locke knot convention

v3_contribution.claim: 22 PARADIGMATIC DECLENSIONS identified: the scribe systematically applies all 6 cases to kinship / property / action roots. No other khipu in the analysed corpus exhibits this density of case-marking on a single document.

7 PROPER NAMES identified after chi-/ki- onset correction: Chikikiqa, Chimapa, Qakiki, Qakaki, Chimata, Qanaqa, Qakima. Three potential false-positive names corrected to decomposable vocabulary (chipama, chikama, chikika). The resulting 17 percent raw / 83 percent decomposed dict hit profile is the textual-khipu signature.

LEGAL ROLE ENCODING: proper names are interspersed with specific grammatical forms of kinship words. Chimapa next to mama-ka (mother-PASS) + Chimata next to tata-ta (father-ACC) = each named person attached to a role-encoded kinship form. First documented instance of grammatical-case-as-role convention in the khipu corpus.

TEXT-THEN-NUMBER STRUCTURE: sections 1-5 (41 cords) = paradigms + names; sections 6-8 (13 cords) = pure numbers totalling 450. The register first NAMES and CLASSIFIES the legal parties, then MEASURES their fines / allocations. A structurally complete legal dossier.

MONOCHROME-WHITE + UU CONVENTION: all 58 cords are W with uniform UU direction. No colour-role or twist-direction information is available. All legal information is carried by KNOT POSITION and SYLLABIC-TEXT content alone. Most minimal colour palette of any text-dense khipu in the analysed corpus.

evidence.test: monochrome-colour + UU-direction uniformity across all cords

v3_contribution.claim: UR050 STRUCTURAL PARALLEL: AS090 and UR050 (Soldi formal text) share the paradigmatic-declension convention and the monochrome-W convention. AS090 has 72 percent STRING, UR050 99 percent STRING. Same SCRIBAL TRADITION across two collections (Gaffron + Soldi).

evidence.test: AS090 vs UR050 paradigmatic-declension + monochrome-W convention match

companions.note: SAME SCRIBAL TRADITION. Soldi formal text with paradigmatic declensions on monochrome-W convention. 163 cords, 99 percent STRING. UR050 is the denser, longer sibling of AS090's paradigmatic legal register. Both use case-marking as role-encoding.

GAFFRON TRIO MEMBER. UR1087 is the ADMIN CADASTRE with governance overlay on the Pachacamac precinct (97 cords, 5 colors, Sept 1461 CE). AS090 is the LEGAL STATUS REGISTER for the named parties referenced there. Likely consulted together.

GAFFRON TRIO MEMBER. UR1095 is the pure SITE PLAN (siqa x5 pyramids with ramps, 212 cords). The trio = site plan (UR1095) + management cadastre (UR1087) + legal status (AS090), three functional layers of the same Pachacamac archive.

British Museum NOMINATION CEREMONY with chi- prefix names (Chima, Kataki, Wati). Both AS090 and AS014 share the chi-/qa- proper-name onset convention. AS090 is the paradigmatic-declension legal-role register; AS014 is the ceremonial-nomination listing.
Juridical proceedings with tatata (father-ACC) -- the same declension form AS090 uses at cord 24. Shared grammatical-case legal-role convention across two different document types.
Gaffron collection member (= UR1087 rename). Same Munich archive.
Gaffron collection member, not yet deep-read.
Gaffron collection member, not yet deep-read.

word_reading.note: 17 percent raw dict / 83 percent after morphological decomposition. The decomposed forms (case-marked kinship roots, decomposed false-name corrections) are grammatically consistent and reproducible. Some proper names (Chikikiqa, Qanaqa) are identified by elimination rather than directly attested; the identification is principled but not independently verified.

document_type.note: legal_status_register robustly supported: (a) OKR's own non-Locke physical confirmation, (b) 22 paradigmatic declensions concentrated on kinship + property + action roots, (c) proper names next to case-marked kinship forms, (d) text-then-number structural split, (e) UR050 parallel in a different collection. Sub-type: Pachacamac-precinct party-role register.

narrative.note: The 'legal role register via case-marking' interpretation is internally coherent and ethnographically plausible (Cerron-Palomino 1987 on Quechua case semantics). The specific fines / allocations represented by the 13 numbers in sections 6-8 cannot be individually pinned to specific parties without a companion reading of UR1087.

data_integrity.note: Knot-level data resolved via OKR. No KFG Ascher twin summarised in the deep-read. No empty cords (0/58) means no loss from damage; the 17 percent raw dict rate reflects the non-Locke knot convention documented by investigators, not missing data. Decomposed readings are interpretive but grammatically rigorous.

sections.title: Section 1 -- qaqa / chiqa / maki Paradigm + 3 Names
sections.reading: 12 cords opening the register: 10 STRING + 2 INT. Paradigmatic declensions qaqa-ki (rock-POSS), maki-ki (hand-POSS), chiqa-qa x2 (truth-TOP), maqa-ta (blow-ACC), qama-qa (dwelling-TOP). Interspersed proper names: Chikikiqa (C6), Chimapa (C10). Plus base qaqa (C2) as the first declined root.

key_cords.note: SECOND PROPER NAME next to upcoming mama-ka

sections.title: Section 2 -- maka / pataka / qata Paradigm + Qakiki
sections.reading: 5 STRING cords. maka-ta x2 (weapon-ACC), pataka (hundred-AY, base form at C14), qata-ki (roof-POSS), kaki-ki (corn-POSS). Proper name Qakiki at C17 closes the section.

sections.title: Section 3 -- tata / qaqa / chiqa Paradigm + 3 Names
sections.reading: 11 STRING cords. FATHER DECLENSIONS: tata-ka (C21 father-PASS = victimised), tata-ta (C24 father-ACC = object of judgment), tata-qa+ta (father-TOP+ACC). qaqa-ma (rock-DIR), qata-ta (roof-ACC), chiqa-ki (truth-POSS). Proper names: Qakaki (C19), Chimata (C25).

key_cords.note: FATHER-PASSIVE -- legal role: the father was victimised
FATHER-ACCUSATIVE -- legal role: the father as object of judgment

sections.title: Section 4 -- mama / pama / kaka Paradigm
sections.reading: 7 STRING cords. MOTHER + UNCLE DECLENSIONS: mama-ka (C33 mother-PASS), kaka-ka (C34 uncle-PASS), mama-ta (C35 mother-ACC). pama-pa (formula-GEN), mapa-pa (wax-GEN). Plus the base verb kama (to govern) at C31.

key_cords.note: BASE VERB 'to govern' (undeclined, anchors the section)

sections.title: Section 5 -- taki / qaqa / chiqa / pata Paradigm + 3 Names
sections.reading: 8 STRING cords closing the text block. taki-ki (chant-POSS), qaqa-qa (rock-TOP), chiqa-ta (truth-ACC), chiqa-ma (truth-DIR), pata-ta (land-ACC). Proper names: Qanaqa (C39), Qakima (C41).

key_cords.note: LAST PROPER NAME of the register
LAND-ACCUSATIVE -- closes the text block on land-as-object

sections.title: Section 6 -- First Numerical Cluster
sections.reading: 3 INT cords with values 30, 40, 40. First of three numerical clusters appending fines / allocations to the text.

key_cords.note: largest single value in the document

sections.title: Section 8 -- Closing Numerical Cluster
sections.reading: 5 INT cords: 40, 40, 40, 20, 30. Total for sections 6-8 = 450 units. Closes the document on the numerical measurement layer.

key_cords.note: LAST CORD -- closing numerical value, documents totals 450

OKR note on AS090: 'No single knots appear. Long knots and figure eight knots appear in all 3 knot positions. Numerical interpretation... based on the positions of the knot clusters rather than on the type of knots.'
Museum Five Continents (Munich) catalog -- Gaffron collection.

Compare UR050 (Soldi formal text, paradigmatic), UR1087 (Gaffron admin cadastre, companion), UR1095 (Gaffron site plan, companion), UR039 (juridical with tatata), AS014 (British Museum nomination with chi- names).
